briefing.title: Sell-Through Lead vs. Market
briefing.detail: Purse Maison leads all stores with 29 units sold MTD (₱8.26M revenue); nearest competitor Miss Manila Luxe sold 26 units but at only ₱1.12M — confirming Purse Maison's superior ticket mix and velocity at ₱180,981 avg vs. their ₱78,726.
briefing.action: Use this momentum to push hero Hermès models actively — Birkin and Kelly are already top sellers; prioritize sourcing replenishment now to sustain July's pace.

briefing.title: Birkin & Kelly Pricing Gap — Risk of Overpricing
briefing.detail: Purse Maison's lowest Birkin 25 ask is ₱1,288,000 vs. market low of ₱950,000 (+35.6%); Birkin 30 is ₱770,000 vs. market low ₱575,000 (+33.9%); Kelly 25 is ₱1,150,000 vs. ₱850,000 (+35.3%) and Kelly 28 is ₱735,000 vs. ₱480,000 (+53.1%).
briefing.action: Audit the specific entry-level SKUs driving these gaps — if condition/hardware is comparable to market low listings, bring entry prices within 10–15% of market floor to capture budget-sensitive buyers without racing to the bottom.

briefing.title: Constance 24 Undercut by Market
briefing.detail: Purse Maison's lowest Constance 24 ask is ₱534,500 vs. market low of ₱395,000 — a ₱139,500 (35.3%) premium on the entry unit, with 7 live units sitting in inventory.
briefing.action: Re-price the lowest 1–2 Constance 24 units to ₱430,000–₱450,000 to become competitive without matching the floor, and flag remaining units as 'premium grade' with explicit hardware/condition callouts.

briefing.title: Inventory Concentration Risk — Hermès Heavy
briefing.detail: Hermès represents 40.5% of Purse Maison's brand mix (₱117.9M est. value) while Louis Vuitton is only 6.1%; LV is actively selling at Miss Manila Luxe (top seller), A&J Luxury, and The Orange Box this month.
briefing.action: Source 5–10 LV entry-to-mid pieces (Neverfull, Speedy, Alma) in the ₱80K–₱180K range to capture the faster-moving accessible luxury segment and diversify sell-through risk.

briefing.title: Chanel Double Flap Jumbo — Largest Exposed Position
briefing.detail: Purse Maison holds 23 Jumbo units — the largest single-model inventory in the snapshot — with a lowest ask of ₱188,000 already matching the market floor; market has 36 live units total, signaling heavy supply.
briefing.action: Pause new Jumbo acquisitions immediately; for units priced above ₱350,000, evaluate a 5–8% markdown this week to accelerate turns before month-end.

briefing.title: Zero Aging 90+ Days — Healthy but Watch Macro Position
briefing.detail: Purse Maison shows 0 units aged 90+ days on ₱291M inventory, a clean aging profile, but avg days-to-sell of 10.6 days against a 1,608-unit stock implies months of forward cover that warrants monitoring.
briefing.action: Set a 60-day aging flag on any unit purchased before May 1 and prepare tiered markdown triggers (5% at day 60, 10% at day 75) to prevent aging from accumulating silently.

alerts: Luxury Wish (159 units, ₱47.26M) and Canone Bags Prime (1,151 units, ₱38.35M) show ZERO sales MTD — possible pricing stagnation or platform issues; monitor for distress pricing that could undercut the market.

The Orange Box holds ₱158.5M inventory with only 6 inferred sales (₱337K MTD) — at ₱225K avg ticket, their Hermès-heavy (53.8%) stock is moving very slowly; watch for potential markdowns that pressure mid-tier Hermès prices.

Mini Kelly (Kelly 20): only 4 units live market-wide; Purse Maison's lowest ask ₱1,540,000 sits above the ₱970,000 market low but below the ₱1,680,000 ceiling — scarcity is real, hold or raise price on the stronger unit.

Miss Manila Luxe is the highest volume competitor by unit count (26 sold) in the accessible luxury segment (₱78K avg ticket) — their LV, Chanel Coco Handle, and Gucci Marmont velocity signals strong demand for sub-₱150K pieces that Purse Maison currently under-serves.

Chanel Double Flap Medium: 31 units live market-wide with market low already at ₱245,900; Purse Maison's floor is ₱280,000 (+13.9%) — with 13 units held, competitive pressure is building and a price response may be needed within 7 days.

pricing.suggestion: Purse Maison entry at ₱1,288,000 is 35.6% above the ₱950,000 market floor; reprice the lowest-grade unit to ₱1,050,000–₱1,100,000 to stay competitive while preserving premium on higher-condition pieces up to ₱3,278,000.
Entry ask of ₱770,000 is 33.9% above the ₱575,000 market low; bring the weakest unit down to ₱620,000–₱650,000 and hold the remaining 3 units at current or higher tiers based on hardware and color.

At ₱735,000 entry vs. a ₱480,000 market floor (53.1% premium) with 11 units live and 28 market-wide, this is the most exposed Kelly position — reprice 2–3 entry units to ₱520,000–₱560,000 to drive turns on lower-grade inventory.
Entry at ₱534,500 is ₱139,500 above market low (₱395,000); reprice 1–2 units to ₱430,000–₱450,000 to capture deal-seeking buyers while maintaining ₱534,500+ on documented pristine or exotic units.

Entry already matches the ₱188,000 market floor across 23 units in an oversupplied 36-unit market; hold entry price but apply a 7% markdown (≈₱175,000) to units priced ₱300,000+ that have been live over 45 days to prevent aging.

Entry at ₱280,000 is 13.9% above the ₱245,900 market low with 13 units held; reprice the bottom 2 units to ₱255,000–₱260,000 to remain in the competitive band and protect sell-through velocity on this high-inventory model.
